title: Activity heat strip across user's day
design_brief: Building daily activity strip (think Apple Screen Time, Wakatime). Pain point: hour-by-hour activity hard to read.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dim hours indistinguishable from quiet hours
ux_reasoning: Horizontal strip showing 24 hours. Each minute as 1px. Color saturation = activity intensity. Hovering reveals specific events.

design_tokens.spacing: Strip: 1440px (1px per min) on desktop; scaled.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Activity heat strip across user's day, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Activity heat strip across user's day: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Hover: shows event details at that minute.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Activity heat strip across user's day through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 1px-per-minute density. 2) Intensity via oklch lightness — perceptually uniform. 3) Hover-to-detail tooltip.

title: Move-row keyboard shortcut (Alt+Up/Down) in lists
design_brief: Building reorderable lists with keyboard (think Notion, Linear). Pain point: drag only.
ux_reasoning: Alt+Up/Down moves focused row up/down. Visual feedback via FLIP animation.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Move-row keyboard shortcut (Alt+Up/Down) in lists, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Move-row keyboard shortcut (Alt+Up/Down) in lists.
design_tokens.motion: Reorder: 200ms FLIP.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Move-row keyboard shortcut (Alt+Up/Down) in lists: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: For Move-row keyboard shortcut (Alt+Up/Down) in lists, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Alt+arrow standard (matches Notion). 2) Hook-based (composable). 3) Keeps focus on moved row.

title: Sticky notes board with collaborator color borders
design_brief: Building shared sticky-notes wall (think Miro, FigJam). Pain point: hard to see who added what.
ux_reasoning: Each note has subtle 2px top border in author's color. Hover note: avatar + name overlay.

design_tokens.motion: Hover lift 200ms.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Sticky notes board with collaborator color borders: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Each note has author-color top border.
Hover: author avatar + name in corner.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Sticky notes board with collaborator color borders through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Color border attribution. 2) Author info on hover (not always cluttering). 3) Real textarea inline.

title: Reaction picker emoji-grid with skin-tone variants
design_brief: Building emoji picker (think Slack picker). Pain point: lack of skin-tone selection.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic skin-tone picker hidden in settings
ux_reasoning: Long-press / right-click an emoji that supports skin-tone: small variant picker appears.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Reaction picker emoji-grid with skin-tone variants, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Reaction picker emoji-grid with skin-tone variants, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Reaction picker emoji-grid with skin-tone variants: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Right-click (or long-press) emoji: skin-tone variants popover.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Reaction picker emoji-grid with skin-tone variants through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Right-click discovery. 2) 6 skin-tone variants (Fitzpatrick scale). 3) Native Unicode (no images).

title: Cohort analysis with retention curve overlay
design_brief: Building retention dashboard (think Amplitude, Mixpanel). Different from heatmap; this is line chart per cohort.
ux_reasoning: Line chart with multiple cohort curves overlaid. Each cohort = one colored line. X = days since join. Y = retention %.

design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Cohort analysis with retention curve overlay, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Cohort analysis with retention curve overlay: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: X = days since join, Y = retention %.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Cohort analysis with retention curve overlay through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Curves enable trend comparison across cohorts. 2) Color gradient for recency. 3) Compact (one chart for N cohorts).

title: Wallet connect with status visualization
design_brief: Building crypto wallet connect (think Coinbase Wallet, MetaMask sign-in). Pain point: connect flow is confusing.
ux_reasoning: Connect button shows 3 states: idle, connecting (animated dots), connected (truncated address). Click connected: shows full address + disconnect.

design_tokens.motion: Dots bounce during connecting.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Wallet connect with status visualization: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Wallet connect with status visualization through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three states with distinct colors. 2) Truncated address (web3 convention). 3) Click-to-disconnect.

title: Dependent dropdowns with smart cascading reset
design_brief: Building cascading selects (think country → state → city). Pain point: changing parent doesn't reset children.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Children show stale options after parent change
No loading state for async children
ux_reasoning: Changing parent resets children. Children fetch options based on parent. Optimistic preserve if child value valid in new context.

design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Dependent dropdowns with smart cascading reset.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Dependent dropdowns with smart cascading reset, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Dependent dropdowns with smart cascading reset: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Parent changes: child resets if previous value not valid for new parent.
Child fetches options async based on parent.
Loading state on child during fetch.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Dependent dropdowns with smart cascading reset through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Optimistic preserve of child if value valid. 2) Loading state visible. 3) Disabled state for child without parent.

title: Particle field background that responds to cursor
design_brief: Building marketing hero with subtle interactive bg (think Vercel, Stripe). Pain point: most particle bg are heavy or static.
ux_reasoning: ~300 particles in WebGL. Cursor creates subtle force field that pushes nearby particles. Frameloop on demand.

design_tokens.motion: 60fps when in viewport.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Particle field background that responds to cursor: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Particle field background that responds to cursor through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cursor force field via WebGL. 2) Particle count bounded (300). 3) Behind content (-z).

title: Bottom nav on mobile becomes hover drawer on desktop
design_brief: Building secondary nav (think Mail folders). Pain point: mobile bottom nav awkward on desktop.
ux_reasoning: Mobile: bottom bar with 4 icons. Desktop: edge-of-screen icon strip that expands to drawer on hover.

design_tokens.motion: Drawer expand 200ms.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Bottom nav on mobile becomes hover drawer on desktop: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Desktop: 56px icon strip, expands to 200px drawer on hover.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Bottom nav on mobile becomes hover drawer on desktop through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hover-expand desktop nav. 2) Mobile bottom bar. 3) One source of truth.

title: Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only)
design_brief: Building dev-time heading validator (think a11y linter). Pain point: people skip heading levels (h1 → h3) which breaks SR nav.
ux_reasoning: Dev-only React component wraps children, scans DOM for heading hierarchy violations, console.warn on each.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only), checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only).
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only), disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only): readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Dev: scans DOM, warns on skipped levels.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Heading hierarchy validator (h1 → h2 → h3 only) through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Dev-only check (zero prod cost). 2) Catches a common a11y bug. 3) Logs offending element for inspection.

title: Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages
design_brief: Building hover-prefetch (think Next.js Link prefetch). Pain point: navigation feels slow.
ux_reasoning: On hover for 200ms, prefetch the link's HTML (or React route data). By click, response is cached.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Subsequent click: page is in cache.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Cache-warm on hover for likely-next pages through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 200ms intent threshold (no waste on quick-pass). 2) Once per URL. 3) Native link rel='prefetch'.

title: Magazine-style image-led product page
design_brief: Building e-commerce product page (think SSENSE, MR PORTER). Pain point: most product pages are 2-col grids with images on side.
ux_reasoning: Editorial: large hero image, type-led product info inline below, accent serif headline, generous whitespace. Image at 4:5 ratio (fashion editorial).

design_tokens.typography: Serif headline 32px, sans 14px.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Magazine-style image-led product page, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Category in caps tracking-widest above title.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Magazine-style image-led product page through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Hero image first, not side. 2) Serif voice. 3) Hard border button (editorial commerce convention).

title: Compact event log with severity indent
design_brief: Building event/audit log (think Stripe events, CloudTrail). Pain point: dense logs without scannability.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Flat list with no severity hint
ux_reasoning: Each event: severity indent (info: 0, warn: 8px, error: 16px) + timestamp (right) + actor + verb. Indentation visually signals severity.

design_tokens.colors: Severity: info gray, warn yellow, error red.
design_tokens.typography: Mono 12px.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Compact event log with severity indent, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Timestamp left, then actor, then verb.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Compact event log with severity indent through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Indent as severity signal (skip-scanable). 2) Compact 24px rows. 3) Color + indent = double-encoded severity.

title: Cmd+Enter to submit, Esc to cancel everywhere
design_brief: Building form-shortcut consistency (think Linear, Slack). Pain point: forms have different submit/cancel shortcuts.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+Enter submits any form regardless of focus inside. Esc closes/cancels. Hints displayed in footer.

design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Cmd+Enter to submit, Esc to cancel everywhere, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Cmd+Enter to submit, Esc to cancel everywhere: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Cmd+Enter to submit, Esc to cancel everywhere through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Universal pattern across forms. 2) Hints displayed always. 3) Works from any focus.

title: Live presence breadcrumb in route path
design_brief: Building presence visible in route breadcrumb (think Linear's 'who's viewing this'). Pain point: presence often lost when navigating.
ux_reasoning: Breadcrumb at top shows route path PLUS small avatar stack of users currently viewing this page.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Live presence breadcrumb in route path, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Live presence breadcrumb in route path, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Live presence breadcrumb in route path: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Right side: avatar stack of present users.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Live presence breadcrumb in route path through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Presence integrated with breadcrumb. 2) Right-aligned avatars. 3) Updates per route.

title: Animated number counter with smart unit jumps
design_brief: Building stat counter (think Stripe homepage stats). Pain point: counts up linearly which looks fake.
ux_reasoning: Counter eases from initial to target value over 1500ms with cubic-bezier. Formats with locale (1,234,567).

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Animated number counter with smart unit jumps, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.typography: Tabular-nums.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Animated number counter with smart unit jumps.

interaction_details: Counts up to target with cubic ease-out.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Animated number counter with smart unit jumps through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Eased animation (not linear). 2) Tabular-nums for stable width. 3) Custom formatter for currency/units.

title: Diverging bar chart for survey results (likert)
design_brief: Building survey/likert results (think Pew Research). Pain point: stacked bar chart hides positive/negative split.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Stacked bar all to one side
ux_reasoning: Each row: bar centered at midpoint. Negative responses (disagree) extend left; positive (agree) extend right.

design_tokens.colors: Strongly disagree: oklch(0.55 0.18 25). Slightly: oklch(0.7 0.12 25). Neutral: oklch(0.85 0.005 250). Slightly agree: oklch(0.7 0.12 145). Strongly: oklch(0.55 0.14 145).
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Diverging bar chart for survey results (likert), disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Diverging bar chart for survey results (likert): readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Bar centered at midpoint between disagree/agree.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Diverging bar chart for survey results (likert) through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Diverging design — positive/negative split obvious. 2) Center line reference. 3) Same total width = same scale.

title: Inline tutorial annotations using popovers anchored to real elements
design_brief: Building product walk-through (think Intercom Articles, Pendo). Pain point: modal walkthroughs interrupt.
ux_reasoning: Each tutorial step is a popover anchored to a real UI element with arrow. Skip available. Step order optional.

design_tokens.motion: Fade-in 200ms.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Inline tutorial annotations using popovers anchored to real elements: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Inline tutorial annotations using popovers anchored to real elements through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Anchored to real elements (no overlay). 2) Always skippable. 3) Step counter for context.

title: Auto-saving form with explicit unsaved indicator
design_brief: Building auto-save form (think Notion, Linear). Pain point: users worry about losing work.
ux_reasoning: Form auto-saves on blur OR debounced 1500ms after last edit. Status indicator: 'Saved · 12s ago' OR 'Saving...' OR 'Editing...'.

design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Auto-saving form with explicit unsaved indicator, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Auto-saving form with explicit unsaved indicator: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Status indicator cycles: editing → saving → saved.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Auto-saving form with explicit unsaved indicator through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Three-state visible cycle. 2) Relative-time freshness. 3) Hook + display separated.

title: 3D card flip on hover for hero showcase
design_brief: Building featured product card with 3D flip (think Apple homepage cards). Pain point: most card flips look gimmicky.
ux_reasoning: Subtle 3D rotation that follows mouse position. Back content reveals only at specific tilt.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for 3D card flip on hover for hero showcase, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.motion: Tilt: 1:1 with cursor.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for 3D card flip on hover for hero showcase: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Past 20deg Y rotation: back becomes visible.
accessibility_considerations: Expose 3D card flip on hover for hero showcase through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cursor-tracking 3D rotation. 2) Back reveal at threshold. 3) backfaceVisibility for clean flip.

title: Search results: 'See all' on mobile vs grouped on desktop
design_brief: Building search categorized results (think Spotlight, Apple search). Pain point: same layout fails one or the other.
ux_reasoning: Desktop: grouped by category (Songs, Artists, Albums) with 3-4 per group + 'See all'. Mobile: vertical list, tap category chip to filter.

design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Search results: 'See all' on mobile vs grouped on desktop, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Search results: 'See all' on mobile vs grouped on desktop: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Desktop: grouped with section headers + 'See all'.
Mobile: filter chips at top, flat list below.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Search results: 'See all' on mobile vs grouped on desktop through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Modality-appropriate categorization. 2) Mobile chips for filter, desktop sections. 3) See-all desktop link.

title: Form errors announced one at a time
design_brief: Building error announcement (think gov.uk). Pain point: multiple errors all announced at once = confusion.
ux_reasoning: On submit, announce error summary count + jump to first. After fixing, announce next.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Form errors announced one at a time, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Form errors announced one at a time.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Form errors announced one at a time, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Form errors announced one at a time: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Focus jumps to first error field.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Form errors announced one at a time through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sequential announcement reduces cognitive load. 2) Auto-focus first error. 3) Count for context.

title: useTransition for non-blocking state updates
design_brief: Using React 18 useTransition (think filter inputs). Pain point: heavy filter updates block input.
ux_reasoning: useTransition marks heavy state updates as low priority. Input stays responsive; isPending shows brief state.

design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for useTransition for non-blocking state updates.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for useTransition for non-blocking state updates, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for useTransition for non-blocking state updates: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Typing in input: input never lags.
Filtered list updates marked as transition.
isPending shows dim list during update.
accessibility_considerations: Expose useTransition for non-blocking state updates through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) React 18 useTransition primitive. 2) Input stays at 60fps. 3) Dim list signals processing.

title: Terminal-style dashboard with green-on-black
design_brief: Building dev-tool UI in terminal aesthetic (think old PuTTY, IRC clients). Pain point: dev tools default to generic dark.
ux_reasoning: Black bg + phosphor-green text. Monospace everywhere. Cursor blink. Subtle CRT scanline overlay.

design_tokens.typography: 'JetBrains Mono' 13px.
design_tokens.motion: Cursor blink 1s.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Terminal-style dashboard with green-on-black through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Period-correct CRT scanline. 2) Blinking cursor (steps 2). 3) ASCII separators.

title: Sortable, filterable, paginated table primitive
design_brief: Generic table primitive (think TanStack Table, AG Grid). Pain point: most basic tables lack the full set.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Heavy library required for simple needs
ux_reasoning: Lightweight composable table with sort/filter/page built in. ~200 LOC. Generic over row type.

design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Sortable, filterable, paginated table primitive, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Sortable, filterable, paginated table primitive: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Filterable columns: input in header row.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Sortable, filterable, paginated table primitive through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Lightweight composable. 2) Per-column filter inputs. 3) Lightweight pagination.

title: Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope
design_brief: Building scoped command palette (think VS Code Cmd+Shift+P vs Cmd+P). Pain point: one palette for everything is overwhelming.
ux_reasoning: Cmd+Shift+P opens action palette (commands only, not files). Cmd+P opens file/page palette. Two distinct surfaces.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Cmd+Shift+P open command palette in scope through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Scoped palettes — focus per context. 2) VS Code convention. 3) Hook composable.

title: Activity bell with count and dropdown
design_brief: Building activity dropdown (think GitHub bell). Pain point: dropdown is messy.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dropdown that closes on click inside
ux_reasoning: Bell icon with count badge. Click: dropdown with last 20 notifications grouped by day. Mark-all-read button.

design_tokens.motion: Open 100ms.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Activity bell with count and dropdown: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

accessibility_considerations: Expose Activity bell with count and dropdown through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Count abbreviation (9+). 2) Mark-all-read inline. 3) Last 20 cap.

title: Onboarding checklist with collapsible state
design_brief: Building setup checklist (think Github 'Get started', Linear setup). Pain point: long checklists feel daunting.
ux_reasoning: Card with progress bar at top + collapsible task list. Each task: check + label + 'Start' button. Auto-collapse on completion.

design_tokens.motion: Strikethrough on check 200ms.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Onboarding checklist with collapsible state: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Header shows X of Y count.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Onboarding checklist with collapsible state through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Disappears at 100% — no nagging. 2) Per-task Start button. 3) Linear progress bar.

title: Slope chart for before/after comparison
design_brief: Building slope chart (think rank comparison between two periods). Pain point: bar charts hide direction.
ux_reasoning: Slope chart: two vertical axes (start / end), each item plotted at its value on both, line connects. Slope = change.

design_tokens.colors: Up: green. Down: red. Flat: gray.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Slope chart for before/after comparison, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Slope chart for before/after comparison: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Color: up green / down red / flat gray.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Slope chart for before/after comparison through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Slope direction = direction of change. 2) Color encodes direction. 3) Labels at both ends.

title: Welcome email with deep-link to specific feature
design_brief: Building post-signup email (think Linear, Vercel). Pain point: generic 'Welcome!' email.
ux_reasoning: Email has 2-3 specific actions ('Create your first project →', 'Invite a teammate →') as deep links that auto-do the action in-app.

design_tokens.typography: Sans-serif.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Welcome email with deep-link to specific feature, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Click CTA: deep-link opens app already in that flow.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Welcome email with deep-link to specific feature through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Deep links bypass app navigation. 2) Source tracking for attribution. 3) 2-3 actions, not 10.
